mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate decline, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the threshold effect loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts signals robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions indicates capacity constraints and rising input costs, which compress margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of late-cycle acceleration periods, or the mechanism fails to predict returns outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation crossing above 2.5 percent forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in >55% of high-inflation months, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts corporate revenues and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent during post-shock recovery phases validates economic stabilization, reducing tail risk and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of high-GDP recovery quarters, or the signal shows no predictive power outside the recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding corporate output and earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following industrial production growth, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling windows.
